description: A startup founder is raising a seed round from a venture investor. The founder wants to preserve ownership and control; the investor wants terms that protect the fund. Negotiate a term sheet that settles ALL of the following, coining shared terms as needed: (1) the valuation the round is priced at, (2) how much is invested and the resulting ownership, (3) the liquidation preference, (4) board composition and who holds seats, and (5) the size of the employee option pool and who it dilutes. Both agents must explicitly agree before the term sheet is final.
